scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify deblur_system_call with an optional num_threads parameter adds --num-threads flag to the command.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that the function correctly includes the --num-threads flag when num_threads is provided.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: When num_threads=4, the command list must contain '--num-threads', '4'.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution that ignores num_threads will not include the flag, or will include it with wrong value.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
input_fp = 'test_sequences.fasta'
with open(input_fp, 'w') as f:
    f.write(">seq1\nACTG\n>seq2\nTGCA\n")
params = ['--min-size', '50']
num_threads = 4
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
stdout, stderr, returncode = deblur_system_call(params, input_fp, num_threads)
print('STDOUT:', stdout)
print('STDERR:', stderr)
print('Return code:', returncode)
result = {'stdout': stdout, 'stderr': stderr, 'returncode': returncode}
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a case that provides num_threads and checks the command structure includes '--num-threads' with the correct value.
Increase input complexity by adding an optional integer parameter `num_threads` that influences command construction.
